detail_desc:
Reilly doesn't keep this inn open by sitting and watching the goblins invade!
He looks more like a hobo than an innkeeper but you can at least see that he is
wearing a huge Scotch sword at his side.  He is also drinking from a huge bottle
of Scotch whiskey, probably not the single-malt variety.
long_desc: The innkeeper watches for customers and drinks imported Scotch.

detail_desc:
Pater Vinci did not bear a weapon in the good old days, but now the goblins
ought to avoid him, because the good priest fought for King Korbor before he
took holy orders, and still carries a sturdy club.
long_desc: Pater Vinci greets you as you pass by.

detail_desc:
You had better keep on Zeb's good side.  He has few qualms about roughing up
the Midgaarders, and is ever-vigilant for evil.
long_desc: Zeb Rykalp, sheriff of Ofingia, watches you closely.

detail_desc:
He is too strong for you to beat easily in a fight, and if he had a beer or
two in him Dorig would be invincible.  At least, he would think himself
invincible.
long_desc: Dorig the dwarf welds bars together with a 10 pound hammer.

detail_desc:
That axe he has has a long spike opposite its blade, and you do not think it
is intended for wood, but for flesh and bone, particularly of goblin-kind.
long_desc: Joel Kreb, the village carpenter, rests with an axe close at hand.

detail_desc:
Guard your wallet carefully!  Rumor has it that Maxtrum rarely runs out of
money, which would be wonderful, if only his customers didn't so often find
their pockets lighter after leaving his store, though they bought nothing.
long_desc: A stout halfling shopkeeper eyes your moneybag with a glinting eye.

detail_desc:
Churt notices you gagging as you enter his shop and peers at you with some
surprise.  He is a powerful wizard, having practiced such great spells as
`Churt's Irresistible Retch' and `Word of Command: Go away, I...  ' Well, never
mind that one.
long_desc: An elven alchemist inhales the fumes deeply and sighs happily.

detail_desc:
Dek Shattershock- the name inspires fear in goblin and githyanki warrior
alike!  You ought to be scared, too, you shrimp.
long_desc: A retired elven ranger works here as a leatherworker.

detail_desc:
She is not a noblewoman, but is a lady all the same.  After all, lady means
`loaf-kneader' just as lord means `loaf-ward' in the old Anglo- Saxon tongue.
She has bashed more goblins with her rolling pin than Jewish rye has caraway
seeds.
long_desc: A young Ofintine woman kneads dough here.

detail_desc:
This priestess is an old friend of Dek, Dorig, Churt, and Maxtrum.  They
saved the world from destruction, but there isn't much need for heroes in
peacetime.  You can see Sara's long spear hung on the wall where she can
instantly reach it.
long_desc: A priestess watches you carefully.

detail_desc: He did not reach this rank by staying close by the Newbie Zone.
long_desc: Passing guards raise their visors in salute to Sir Kelek, their captain.

detail_desc:
Once King Korbor sat in his lofty hall, but now he marches with his army to
drive off the goblins which threaten his realm.
long_desc: Clad in bright mail and girt with a sword, King Korbor goes to war.

detail_desc: Guard your money!  It is that dratted Tim!
long_desc: A small boy sneaks silently through the shadows.

detail_desc: This knight could easily kill Curley GreenLeaf.
long_desc: Sir Mischa guards his town from evil creatures.

detail_desc: A sturdy guardsman, loyal to King Korbor.
long_desc: A guard of Ofintinia patrolls his town.

detail_desc: A sturdy guardsman, ever vigilant for evil.
long_desc: An Ofintine guard watches the gate.

detail_desc: He has taken up arms to fight the goblins.
long_desc: An Ofintine volunteer patrolls his town.

detail_desc: She has taken up arms to fight the goblins.
long_desc: A citizen of Ofinitine looks fierce and determined.

detail_desc: He won't desert his town for a million goblins, all waving axes.
long_desc: A man of Ofingia stands here.

detail_desc: Though the goblins poured in through every gate, she would stay here.
long_desc: An Lonoyan woman walks through town.

detail_desc: He is ready for the goblins when they come!
long_desc: A small boy runs around shouting at unseen foes.

detail_desc: You are sure she has killed many trolls and more goblins.
long_desc: A Lonoyan girl stands here.

detail_desc: A big goofy retriever, with a rough, coarse black coat.
long_desc: A huge dog runs about, tongue lolling out of his mouth in a silly way.

detail_desc: Reilly's miniature schnauzer looks tougher than a small dragon.
long_desc: You cringe in fear when a small black dog growls menacingly.

detail_desc:
As he walks past you, you see him stop and stare with an expression of
recognition, changing to anger.  Perhaps you left his gate open on the way here.
long_desc: A farmer strolls about town in his Sunday clothes.

detail_desc:
It takes more than a beer or two to intoxicate the sturdy mountain folk; he
must have had enough to stun a giant.
long_desc: A drunken Dwarf rests here, singing loud enough to wake the dead.

detail_desc:
There aren't too many hobbits living in Lonoya, and he is most likely a
visitor from the Shire.
long_desc: A fine fat gentlehobbit swaggers around, whistling merrily.

detail_desc:
No one bothers her as she serves drinks, because they know that she has a
dagger ready for anyone who gets `fresh'.
long_desc: A lovely Lonoyan woman serves mead to Reilly's customers.

detail_desc:
Too old to wield a glaive, this man has taken upon himself the task of
picking up the litter that passerby drop in Lonoyan gutters.  Ofingia, the
"Jewel of Lonoya", would not sparkle as it does in Korbor's crown if he was not
so busy.  The old man really hates adventurers who don't clean up after
themselves.
long_desc: An old Lonoyan man sweeps the road clean.

detail_desc:
He doesn't seem to notice you right away, but I wouldn't wait for him to stop
talking and see you.  Goblins hate strangers, especially if they are armed.
long_desc: A forest goblin roams Lonoya, arguing with himself.

detail_desc: Stumpy-short but thick, green-skinned and great-headed.  Ugly, ain't he?
long_desc: A goblin raider prowls Lonoya, looking for travellers to rob.

detail_desc: Another goblin, another battle, another notch in your newbie dagger.
long_desc: A goblin crouches in ambush here.

detail_desc: Goblins, it seems, are not trusty watchmen.  You'd expect no more.
long_desc: A goblin stands watch here.

detail_desc:
For a little while you fear poison, but this snake looks about as harmless as
any forty-five-foot reptile can.  Perhaps this serpent lives off the carcasses
of those killed by these goblins.
long_desc: A great serpent slithers through the caverns, ignoring you.

detail_desc: He is wandering, as you are, through these tunnels to find an exit.
long_desc: A stone-gnome is far from home.

detail_desc: No one will pass by him unnoticed.
long_desc: A goblin stands here alert, vigilant for intruders.

detail_desc: He looks tougher than the average goblin.
long_desc: A green-skinned goblin warrior stands here, clutching a bloody scimitar.

detail_desc: He looks tougher than the average goblin.
long_desc: A goblin warrior glares at you, angry at having been awakened.

detail_desc:
You would NEVER wear his armor; untanned cowhide is not your style.  He
doesn't seem to mind it, however, and in any case this fellow is a dangerous foe
for any knight.
long_desc: A large goblin clad in stinking hides glares at you.

detail_desc:
What does he throw?  Rocks, mostly.  Toadstool is taller than a dwarf, and
ugly as a toad.  He is larger than any goblin you have seen, in fact; maybe
Toadstool is orcish or half-orcish.
long_desc: Toadstool, chief of the forest goblins, shouts and throws things at you.

detail_desc: That bone looks very tasty, but he probably won't share it.
long_desc: A goblin gnaws a bone here.

detail_desc:
As you watch, safely hidden, he picks up a dead goblin and throws it into his
pot, followed by a few spotted mushrooms and a halfling.  Then he catches sight
of you, and wonders what flavor you'd add to his concoction.  Oops!  Should have
stayed still, you idiot.
long_desc: A huge hairy troll stirs a bubbling pot.

detail_desc:
This barrel has no tap, but perhaps you could borrow one from the goblins.
No, I doubt it.  Besides, there is something queer about that cask.  Did it just
move?
long_desc: A sturdy oaken barrel bound with iron hoops stands in the corner.

detail_desc:
You nearly open the cabinet when you realize goblins don't write in the
Common Speech.  Perhaps it's a trap.
long_desc: A cabinet marked `Magical Items Only' stands in the corner.

detail_desc: Half-crawler, half-concrete; it could only be the legendary Cementipede!
long_desc: A strange hybrid of stone and centipede prowls the caves.

detail_desc: You don't want to THINK about what he's been drinking.
long_desc: A drunken goblin staggers around.

detail_desc:
The goblins sometimes ride wargs, and maybe this one is trained as a mount.
You only know that the white fangs it now shows as it sees you approaching look
very sharp.
long_desc: A hungry warg stands here, growling at you.

detail_desc: Looks at you?  Molds don't usually have EYES!  But this one does.
long_desc: A peculiar green mold looks at you from the wall.

detail_desc: He is not as large as the usual Orc, probably half a goblin.
long_desc: An Orc guards the caves here.

detail_desc: He doesn't seem to be in the best of spirits.  It's been a long day.
long_desc: A goblin miner chips away at the mountains' stony innards.

detail_desc: You can't see him.
long_desc: The invisible stalker is, fortunately, not after you.

detail_desc: He is always looking for a way to escape.
long_desc: A kobold slave hauls rubble for the goblins.

detail_desc: He is not too tired to kill you with that big nasty pick.
long_desc: A goblin miner rests here, out of his master's sight.

detail_desc: Where did that thing come from?  It didn't just MOVE, did it?  Hmmm...
long_desc: A great boulder nearly blocks the tunnel.

detail_desc:
He lashes fiercely at you with that great whip when he sees you in his mine
tunnels.
long_desc: A goblin slave driver supervises the miners and cracks his whip!

detail_desc:
The goblins captured him in a raid and now work him to death in their huge
mines.
long_desc: An Ofintine works as a goblin slave, with no hope of escape.

detail_desc:
The goblins captured her in a raid and now work her to death in their huge
mines.
long_desc: An Ofintine works as a goblin slave, with no hope of escape.

detail_desc: His knotted, iron-hard muscles attest to long hours at the anvil.
long_desc: Wielding two great hammers, the goblin smith toils at his forge.

detail_desc:
He works at a furious pace as the overseer cracks his whip, to keep the
goblins' iron furnace white-hot.
long_desc: A goblin pumps the great forge-bellows here.

detail_desc: He avoids the whip nimbly as he feeds the roaring furnace.
long_desc: A kobold shuffles quickly about, carrying iron ore and lime.

detail_desc:
He is not very bright, as few giants are, and might think you work here if
you stay much longer.  Like any evil giant, he loves causing pain.
long_desc: A hill giant `encourages' the slaves with a huge flint-studded whip.

detail_desc: This iron pig seems to be chuckling softly.  Odd.
long_desc: A glowing iron pig cools in the sand, away from the others.

detail_desc: He looks very strong.
long_desc: A large ogre trudges about, pushing boulders that must weigh tons.

detail_desc:
He, like you, has crept into Goblin-Town to hunt goblins.  Seems as though he
is doing a good job of it, so why not let him continue?
long_desc: An Elven hunter stalks goblins here.

detail_desc: He can't see you when he's sleeping, and you had best let him stay that way.
long_desc: A large red dragon stands here, angry that you disturbed his rest.

detail_desc: Oh dear!  Time to leave the way you came in!
long_desc: A huge troll of the Olog-Hai stands guard here.

detail_desc:
Offering him a waybread was a BAD idea, you now think.  Orcs don't like elves
very much.  Well, they do like elves dead.  In fact, they like almost any meat.
You probably look like a tasty meal.
long_desc: A large Orc stands here, looking for dinner.

detail_desc:
You have heard of walls that came alive by wicked sorcery and absorb great
treasures within their magical masonry.
long_desc: A section of stone wall has many treasures embedded in it.

detail_desc: The chest was truly the work of a master carpenter.
long_desc: A fine iron-bound chest would catch any cabinetmaker's eye.

detail_desc: Moragd is a great goblin warrior, son of Gorgh the Knob-headed.
long_desc: Moragd the Goblin stands here.

detail_desc:
Gorgh, father of Moragd , is the greatest goblin warrior yet to taint this
world.
long_desc: Gorgh the Knob-Headed stands here.

detail_desc: Khos, Gorgh, and Moragd are the three best soldiers of the Great Goblin.
long_desc: Khos the Greatnosed stands here.

detail_desc: Ugly, slime-green, and bull-strong.
long_desc: A goblin warrior stands here.

detail_desc:
You are sure you have seen Thrash before, somewhere.  Maybe he was in the
Grunting Boar the other day with Pippin.  Anyway, the Great Goblin has hired him
as a bodyguard.
long_desc: Thrash the Ogre Mercenary guards the Great Goblin.

detail_desc:
You are sure you have seen Throm before, somewhere.  Maybe he was at the
weapon shop the other day?  Anyway, the Great Goblin has hired this renowned
giant to guard his person.
long_desc: Throm the Giant guards the Great Goblin.

detail_desc:
You are sure you have seen Tarus before, perhaps on BluemageMUD.  This sturdy
human is one of the Great Goblin's bodyguards.
long_desc: Tarus the Swordpupil guards the Great Goblin.

detail_desc:
His head is bigger than any goblin's you have seen, and he is quite proud of
it.  This goblin is quite strong, and quite ugly, and has quite a few guards
around him.  Kill him, and King Korbor will repay you handsomely.
long_desc: The Great Goblin sees you and calls his guards!
